text: The Ahr signaling pathway has been shown to have critical functions in the heart, including development of the heart and angiogenesis in a variety of organisms (See “Activation, AhR” Key event 18 page for references).     Further, disruption of the Ahr signaling pathway has been associated with cardiovascular toxicity in animals including birds and fish (Heid et al. 2001; Incardona et al. 2009).     This KER page provides some highlights of the evidence relating Ahr activation and cardiovascular development and function.
